title: Immortal Invisible God Only Wise
author: Walter Chalmers Smith
story: Scottish Free Church minister Smith drew on 1 Timothy 1:17 to describe God's transcendent attributes. The Welsh melody 'St. Denio' gives energy to these theological meditations on God's eternal nature.

lines: God only wise, In light inaccessible hid from our eyes
victorious, Thy great Name we praise.
Unresting, unhasting, and silent as light, Nor wanting, nor wasting, Thou rulest in might
Thy justice, like mountains, high soaring above Thy clouds, which are fountains of goodness and love
To all, life Thou givest, to both great and small
In all life Thou livest, the true life of all
We blossom and flourish as leaves on the tree, And wither and perish but naught changeth Thee
Great Father of glory, pure Father of light, Thine angels adore Thee, all veiling their sight
But of Thy rich graces this grace, Lord, impart Take the veil from our face, the vile from our heart
O help us to see 'Tis only the splendor of light hideth Thee, And so let Thy glory, almighty, impart, Through Christ in His story, Thy Christ to the heart
